Name: should statements
Description: Using rigid rules for how things should be, often leading to guilt or frustration.

Name: disqualifying the positive
Description: Ignoring or invalidating positive experiences as irrelevant or flukes.

Name: jumping to conclusions
Description:
Making assumptions without evidence. Includes: 
→ Mind Reading: Believing you know what others are thinking (usually negatively).
→ Fortune Telling: Predicting negative outcomes without proof.

Name: catastrophizing
Description: Expecting the worst-case scenario or exaggerating the importance of problems.

Name: minimization
Description: Downplaying the significance of positive qualities or achievements.

Name: blaming
Description: Attributing all responsibility for negative outcomes to others.

Name: fallacy of fairness
Description: Believing life should always be fair, leading to frustration when it isn’t.

Name: mental filter
Description: Focusing only on the negative details of a situation and filtering out the positives.

Name: fallacy of change
Description: Expecting others to change through pressure or persistence.

Name: overgeneralization
Description: Making broad interpretations from a single event (e.g., “I failed once, so I’ll always fail”).

Name: personalization
Description: Blaming yourself for events beyond your control.

Name: emotional reasoning
Description: Assuming feelings reflect reality (e.g., “I feel worthless, so I must be worthless”).

Name: all-or-nothing thinking
Description: Viewing situations in black-or-white terms, without recognizing nuance or middle ground.

Name: control fallacies
Description:
Beliefs about control that distort responsibility. Includes:
→ External Control Fallacy: Believing others or fate control your life completely.
→ Internal Control Fallacy: Believing you are responsible for everyone else’s feelings or problems.

Name: heaven’s reward fallacy
Description: Believing sacrifices and effort should always result in reward, leading to resentment.

Name: labeling and mislabeling
Description: Assigning negative labels to yourself or others based on limited evidence.
